zh.lifeSummary: 當Walter Smyth Cromwell於1450年出生在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾諾維爾時，他的父親Sir John Cromwell12歲，母親Joan Smythe8歲。他和Catherine Glossop至少有2個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1510年在英國素瑞普特尼享年60歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Walter Smyth Cromwell于1450年出生在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾諾維爾时，他的父亲Sir John Cromwell12岁，母亲Joan Smythe8岁。他和Catherine Glossop至少有2个儿子和3个女儿。他去世于1510年在英国素瑞普特尼享年60岁，埋葬在英国英格兰倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: Cromwell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from a place so named in Nottinghamshire, from Old English crumb ‘bent, crooked’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

nameMeanings.name: Smyth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ronan, Aidan, Brigid, Conor, Eoin, Malachy, Paddy.
English: variant of   Smith  . This form of the surname is particularly common Ireland.

variants.name: Smith
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Walter Smyth Cromwell was born in 1450, in Norwell, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir John Cromwell, was 12 and his mother, Joan Smythe, was 8. He had at least 2 sons and 3 daughters with Catherine Glossop. He died in 1510, in Putney, Surrey, England, at the age of 60, and was buried in London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Walter Smyth Cromwell est né en 1450, à Norwell, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John Cromwell, avait 12 ans et sa mère, Joan Smythe, avait 8 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles avec Catherine Glossop. Il est décédé en 1510, à Putney, Surrey, Angleterre, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Walter Smyth Cromwell 1450 in Norwell, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Cromwell 12 und seine Mutter Joan Smythe 8. Er hatte mit Catherine Glossop mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb 1510 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Putney, Surrey, England und wurde in London, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Walter Smyth Cromwell è nato il nel 1450 nel Norwell, Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir John Cromwell, aveva 12 anni e il suo madre, Joan Smythe, aveva 8 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie con Catherine Glossop. Morì nel 1510, nel Putney, Surrey, England, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolto a London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Walter Smyth Cromwellが1450年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアノーウエルで生まれた時，父Sir John Cromwellは12歳，母Joan Smytheは8歳であった。彼はCatherine Glossopとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1510年にイギリススリープウトニーで60歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Walter Smyth Cromwell(이)가 1450년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 노웰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Cromwell(은)는 12세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Smythe(은)는 8세였다. 그는 Catherine Glossop(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1510년 영국 수리 풋니에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Walter Smyth Cromwell nasceu em 1450, em Norwell, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir John Cromwell, tinha 12 anos e sua mãe, Joan Smythe, tinha 8 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas com Catherine Glossop. Ele faleceu em 1510, em Putney, Surrey, Inglaterra, com 60 anos, e foi sepultado em London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Walter Smyth Cromwell родился в 1450 г. в местности норвелл, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir John Cromwell было 12 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Smythe, 8. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери от Catherine Glossop. Он умер в 1510 г. в местности путни, сурри, Англия в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Walter Smyth Cromwell nació en 1450, en Norwell, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir John Cromwell, tenía 12 años y su mamá, Joan Smythe, tenía 8 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas con Catherine Glossop. Murió en 1510, en Putney, Surrey, Inglaterra, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultado en London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
